what_it_does: Synthesizes a six-territory coverage sweep of the designed corpus (design corpus, substrate, runtime, canvas/FE, cognition, voice) into an integration summary that maps each territory's relation to the Guided Review Surface and proposes merging the vault RHM Walkthrough Organ criteria with the repo synthesis into a single completion criteria.
observations: Declares the Guided Review Surface and the RHM Walkthrough Organ are the same design expressed from two vantage points.
Breaks coverage into six territories, each with Key finding, Use findings, Touch findings, Unify findings, and Relate findings.
Includes a Relation Summary Table summarizing Use/Touch/Unify/Relate per territory.
Provides a Build Dependency Graph: IAS Phase 0 prerequisites, then GRS main build Buckets A/B/C, plus schema fixes.
States the runtime walkthrough engine is already built and ready to wire (suite.py line ranges cited).
Identifies the FE as a 1660-line monolith and IAS Phase 0 (F0 restructure) as a hard prerequisite.
Lists forward-design points: doc:// / area:// grammar extension, mockup-stop tolerance, streaming part grain, reserved slots.
Ends with evidence grounding describing repo, vault, and codebase walks and evidence marks (Observed/Inferred/Unification-opportunity).
standouts: The single largest finding: GRS IS the RHM Walkthrough Organ; vault criteria should become the parent spec.
Runtime walkthrough engine is reported ready (Bucket A), with FE show-me lane as the remaining work.
The merged document is proposed as loop-prep's single completion criteria.
IAS Phase 0 prerequisites gate any parallel GRS build.
conventions: Uses a territory-by-territory coverage structure with consistent Use/Touch/Unify/Relate categories.
Cites internal code locations and scheme names (suite.py:line, contracts/address.py, mockup://, ui://).
Labels work with bucket identifiers (A1-A3, B1-B5, C-a-C-h) and remediation spec tags (T0-WIRE, T0-KEYSTONE, T0-CARD).
Includes evidence-mark taxonomy: Observed(file:line), Inferred, Unification-opportunity.
Uses Markdown tables and an ASCII dependency graph.
concerns: Heavy jargon and acronyms (RHM, IAS, GRS, CC, OCS, COA, etc.) make the file hard to read without prior context.
The claim that the runtime engine is 'fully built and ready to wire' is asserted but not demonstrated inside this file.
The build graph mixes prerequisites, parallel work, and schema fixes without explicit ordering constraints or risk notes.
Forward-design points are labeled 'not blockers' but could expand scope if not bounded.
notes: A prescriptive integration summary arguing that the Guided Review Surface and the RHM Walkthrough Organ should be merged into one completion criteria before build loop-prep.
questions: What is 'loop-prep' and what are its exact acceptance criteria?
Where are the six underlying coverage files and how do they substantiate the 'ready to wire' claim?
What is the current completion status of IAS Phase 0 prerequisites (F0, S0-S5, I1-I6, R1-R2)?
How is the proposed merge of vault criteria and repo synthesis performed in practice?
What are the precise definitions of the RHM Walkthrough Organ criteria A-G?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a neutral coverage integration summary; actual content is a prescriptive unification thesis and build plan that elevates the findings into a single completion criteria.
apparent_themes: Grounded-code proof mapped to vault design intent
Address grammar as the substrate floor
Human-review channel as a central organ
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish that the Guided Review Surface build should become the Company's single unifying human-interaction organ by collapsing the vault walkthrough criteria and the repo synthesis into one implementation target.
novelty: It treats a UI surface not as a panel but as an instantiation of the Sequences Primitive relational loop at human scale, and frames the build as merging two separate specs into one organ rather than satisfying two sibling specs.
contribution: A structured cross-territory relation table and build dependency graph that links design corpus, substrate, runtime, canvas, cognition, and voice into a single implementation plan, plus the unification thesis that vault walkthrough criteria and repo synthesis are one design.
summary_for_embedding: A coverage-integration summary for the Guided Review Surface that maps six design territories and proposes merging the vault RHM Walkthrough Organ criteria with the repo synthesis into a single loop-prep completion criteria.
intention_for_embedding: To align vault and repo specifications before implementation by showing the Guided Review Surface is the same design as the RHM Walkthrough Organ and defining the prerequisite and build structure needed.
